# Data-Integrity Checklist: PRSG-010 Harden the Hatch + O5 Monster Epics

**Purpose**: Validate that PRSG-010 data-integrity requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable before implementation, with emphasis on O5 manifest topology, status rollup completeness, generated MOC/index ownership, and routing JSON vocabulary stability.
**Created**: 2026-06-11
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md) | [plan.md](../plan.md) | [data-model.md](../data-model.md) | [contracts/](../contracts/)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

**Depth**: Formal release gate
**Audience**: Spec author and reviewer
**Focus**: O5 parent manifest deterministic links to flat sibling child specs, no silent omission in status rollups, generated MOC/index zones regenerated only by the existing generator, and stable JSON-valid route signal vocabulary.

## O5 Manifest Topology

- [ ] CHK001 Are O5 parent manifest fields specified as review-visible CONTRACT data with schema version, kind, parent metadata, ordered children, shared design concept, nullable retrospective, and optional declared rollup status? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011]
- [ ] CHK002 Are child identifiers, branch names, and paths specified as flat siblings under `specs/`, with nested `specs/<parent>/<child>` paths explicitly prohibited? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012 §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK003 Are parent and child `path` values required to match their corresponding `branch` values exactly as `specs/<branch>`, and is this required in the topology validator rather than left to a loose schema pattern? [Resolved - Spec §FR-011/FR-012/FR-014, data-model.md, and o5-parent-manifest.schema.json descriptions now require topology-validator branch/path equality]
- [ ] CHK004 Are duplicate child identifiers, missing child specs, unsupported nested paths, unknown dependencies, later-child dependencies, and cycles all required to produce actionable topology problems before status rollup proceeds? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK005 Are zero-child manifests invalid so an O5 parent cannot appear complete without declared child work? [Completeness, Data Model §O5 Parent Manifest]
- [ ] CHK006 Are dependency-order rules tied to manifest order rather than filesystem order, so repeated runs are deterministic? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014 §FR-015]

## Status Rollup Completeness

- [ ] CHK007 Is topology validation required before child status computation, with `invalid_topology` taking precedence over all child states? [Consistency, Spec §FR-015]
- [ ] CHK008 Are child state rows required to be computed and displayed in manifest child order? [Completeness, Spec §FR-015]
- [ ] CHK009 Does the spec require every declared child to appear exactly once in the rollup output, including failed, pending, blocked, archived, or missing-state children? [Resolved - Spec §FR-015 and data-model.md now require exactly one row per declared child in manifest order]
- [ ] CHK010 Are failed and blocked child states defined as higher precedence than in-progress, pending, and complete states? [Clarity, Spec §FR-015]
- [ ] CHK011 Is optional declared rollup status treated only as a drift check, never as the source of truth? [Consistency, Spec §FR-015, Data Model §O5 Status Rollup]
- [ ] CHK012 Does the implementation plan require a mixed-child-state fixture that proves failed, pending, and blocked children are not silently omitted from O5 rollup output? [Resolved - Plan §Split Plan now requires mixed child-state fixtures with every declared child emitted exactly once]

## Generated MOC and Index Integrity

- [ ] CHK013 Are O5 child `SPEC-MOC.md` requirements clear that `up:` remains pointed at the roadmap while curated body links point to the parent manifest and shared design concept? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012]
- [ ] CHK014 Are generated `SPEC-MOC.md` zones required to remain under the existing `generate-spec-index.sh` ownership model rather than being hand-patched by O5 scaffold/status logic? [Resolved - Spec §FR-012, data-model.md, and Plan §Split Plan now require generator-owned zones and generator-based validation]
- [ ] CHK015 Are generated-zone edits separated from curated frontmatter/body-link edits so O5 support does not create stale index or backlink drift? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012 §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK016 Are existing flat `specs/*` scan assumptions preserved without introducing nested child directory scanning? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK017 Are generated map/index validations named as Layer 1 coverage so stale MOC/index drift remains mechanically detectable? [Measurability, Plan §Testing]

## Routing JSON Vocabulary

- [ ] CHK018 Is the routing decision shape specified as a flat JSON object with `route`, `releasable`, `signals`, `hints`, and `warnings` rather than a replacement nested shape? [Consistency, Spec §FR-022]
- [ ] CHK019 Are decisive contextual signal tokens closed and stable for flag-system, release-cadence, consumer-locality, and branch-by-abstraction outcomes? [Completeness, Spec §FR-018 §FR-019 §FR-020, Contract routing-decision.schema.json]
- [ ] CHK020 Are weak, stale, fixture-only, code-fence-only, conflicting, or shallow evidence tokens excluded from `signals[]` and limited to closed-enum `hints[]` values? [Clarity, Spec §FR-021]
- [ ] CHK021 Does the production routing schema validate both success and error decision payloads without allowing undeclared extra fields? [Completeness, Contract routing-decision.schema.json]
- [ ] CHK022 Does the plan require every router fixture output, including contextual-probe success and weak-evidence cases, to validate against the production routing schema so enum drift cannot pass silently? [Resolved - Plan §Split Plan now requires production schema validation for every router fixture output]

## Cross-Artifact Consistency

- [ ] CHK023 Are spec, data model, and JSON contracts consistent on O5 status vocabulary: `invalid_topology`, `blocked`, `failed`, `in_progress`, `pending`, and `complete`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-015, Data Model §O5 Status Rollup, Contract o5-parent-manifest.schema.json]
- [ ] CHK024 Are plan slices ordered so O5 schema/status work and routing schema work land with their own deterministic fixtures before docs/parity polish? [Completeness, Plan §Split Plan]

## Notes

- Gap markers identify missing or under-specified requirements and contracts, not implementation failures.
